author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
title: Extract from Augustin’s Retractations.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Then follow four books which I wrote to Boniface, bishop of the Roman Church, in opposition to two letters of the Pelagians, because when they came into his hands he had sent them to me, finding in them a calumnious mention of my name.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: This work commences on this wise: “I had indeed known you by the praise of your renowned fame.”
